items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, and privilege escalation. Adobe recommends applying the latest security updates as soon as possible.

items.title: File Upload Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: A critical file upload vulnerability (CVSS 9.6) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to privilege escalation. This vulnerability is likely addressed in APSB26-73.

items.title: Improper Encoding/Escaping of Output in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: An improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability (CVSS 9.1) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary code execution. This vulnerability is likely addressed in APSB26-73.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP object injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical (CVSS 9.8) unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, leading to remote code execution. Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities, including issues that could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass. Adobe is not aware of any exploits in the wild.

items.title: Stored XSS leading to arbitrary code execution in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A Stored XSS vulnerability (CVSS 8.7) affecting Adobe Commerce, Adobe Commerce B2B, and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary code execution. This issue is fixed by APSB26-49.

items.title: Path Traversal leading to arbitrary file system write in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A Path Traversal vulnerability (CVSS 8.7) affecting Adobe Commerce, Adobe Commerce B2B, and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary file system write. This issue is fixed by APSB26-49.

items.title: Unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files by disguising code as an image. Adobe released Magento 2.4.9 with a fix, but older supported versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This bulletin addresses multiple critical vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, execute unauthorized malicious code, and compromise store data. Adobe recommends applying security patches quickly.

items.title: Stored XSS leading to privilege escalation in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A Stored XSS vulnerability (CVSS 8.7) requiring admin access could lead to privilege escalation in Adobe Commerce. This is one of the severe issues addressed in APSB26-05.

items.title: Stored XSS leading to privilege escalation in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A Stored XSS vulnerability (CVSS 8.1) could lead to privilege escalation in Adobe Commerce. This is one of the issues addressed in APSB26-05.

items.title: Stored XSS leading to privilege escalation in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A Stored XSS vulnerability (CVSS 8.1) could lead to privilege escalation in Adobe Commerce. This is one of the issues addressed in APSB26-05.
